back_translation: When the Sabbath day had passed, at the dawn of the first day of the week, Mary of Magdala and another Mary came to see the burial tomb.
And behold, a great earthquake occurred, because an angel of the Lord descended [formal] from the sky of heaven, came and rolled the stone away, then sat upon that stone.
The appearance of that angel was like flashing lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
Because of fear of that angel, the guards who were on watch trembled and became like dead people.
But that angel said to the women, 'You all, do not be afraid, for I know that you all are seeking Jesus who was crucified on the cross.
He is not here, because He has been raised [formal] just as He had said [formal]. Come and see the place where He had been laid [formal-passive].

Now go quickly and tell His disciples, ‘He has been raised [formal] from death, and behold, He is going [formal] ahead of you all to the region of Galilee; there you all will see Him.’ Behold, I have told you all.'
They [the women] then hurried out of the burial tomb with fear and great joy, running to tell this news to His disciples.
And behold, Jesus met [formal] them [the women], saying [formal], 'Rejoice.' They [the women] then came and knelt down, embracing His feet [royal] and worshiped Him.
At that time, Jesus said [formal] to them [the women], 'Do not be afraid. Go tell my brothers to go to the region of Galilee, and they will see Me there.'
While they [the women] were going along, behold, some among the guard unit came into the city and reported everything that had happened to the chief priests.
They then met together with the elders, and when they had consulted together, they gave a large sum of money to the soldiers.
And said, 'You all are to say, “His disciples came at night and stole his body away while we were sleeping,”
and if this matter is reported to the governor, we ourselves will persuade him, and we will make you all not have to worry.'
The soldiers then received the money and did according to what they had been taught, and this story spread among the Jews until this very day.
As for the eleven disciples, they went to the region of Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had appointed [formal] them.
When they saw Him, they worshiped Him, but some still doubted.
Jesus came forward [formal] and said [formal] to them, 'All authority in heaven and on the earth has been granted [formal] to Me already.
Therefore, go out and teach all the nations to be disciples of Mine, giving baptism to them in the Name [royal] of the Father [royal], the Son [royal], and the Holy Spirit [royal],
teaching them to keep all things that I have commanded you all. And behold, I will be with you all always until the end of the age.'
